title: watch.rs: path traversal check is TOCTOU (canonicalize then read)
description: watch_loop calls canonicalize(file_path) to verify the path stays within watch_path, then calls process_file which calls tokio::fs::read_to_string(file_path). Between the canonicalize check and the read, an attacker with write access to the watch directory can replace the file with a symlink pointing outside the watch directory. The security comment at line 61-62 claims 'Read the file first, then verify' but the code does the opposite: it canonicalizes (step 3-5), checks containment (step 5), then reads (step 6 via process_file). The TOCTOU is not eliminated. Fix: open the file by canonical path rather than original file_path after verification, or use the canonical path for both check and read.
close_reason: Fixed: watch.rs reads first, then canonicalizes to verify path.
